PROJECT OVERVIEW

About This Project

A full-cycle data engineering and analytical intelligence project built on a real-world Portuguese hotel booking dataset.

Part 1 — Database Engineering8 pts
  • Normalized relational schema — 11 entities, 4 functional domains, 3NF throughout
  • Full SQL Server implementation with PK/FK, CHECK, and UNIQUE constraints
  • 4-stage bulk insert pipeline: Stage → Validate → Normalize → Error-log
  • 10 analytical SQL views: occupancy, revenue, cancellations, housekeeping, staff
  • CRUD operations: INSERT reservations, UPDATE status, DELETE test records
Part 2 — Data Analysis & ML8 pts
  • Full 20-question Appendix B EDA: profiling, quality, univariate, bivariate, multivariate
  • Python: pandas, matplotlib, seaborn — clean notebook with 18 visualizations
  • Random Forest classifier: AUC 0.86, Accuracy 81%, F1 0.79
  • k-Means guest clustering: k=3, Silhouette Score 0.61
  • Orange Data Mining workflows for both supervised and unsupervised learning

DATABASE ENGINEERING

Relational Schema Design

11 normalized tables across 4 functional domains — Reservations as the central hub with 4 foreign keys.

DESIGN PRINCIPLE
Schema normalized to 3NF throughout. Reservations entity acts as a hub with 4 FKs linking Hotels, Guests, Rooms, and Staff. The 1:1 Reservation→Invoice relationship and ServiceUsage junction table deliberately separate operational and billing analytics.
Master Data (6 tables)
  • Hotels — HotelID, Name, City, Country, StarRating
  • RoomTypes — TypeID, Name, Capacity, BasePrice
  • Rooms — RoomID, UNIQUE(HotelID, RoomNum)
  • Guests — GuestID, Name, Email, IsRepeatedGuest
  • Staff — StaffID, Role, Department, HotelID FK
  • Services — ServiceID, Name, Category, Price
Transactional (4 tables)
  • Reservations — 4×FK hub, Status, Channel, Nights
  • ServiceUsage — M:N junction (Reservation × Service)
  • Invoices — 1:1 with Reservation, OutstandingAmount
  • Payments — 1:N under Invoice, partial payments OK
Operational (1) + Staging (1)
  • HousekeepingLogs — StartTime, EndTime, CleaningType
  • HotelBooking_Staging — flat NVARCHAR buffer for CSV ingestion
  • 4-stage ETL: Load → Validate → Normalize → Error-log
  • 119,390 rows ingested in under 30 seconds

10 Analytical SQL Views

#View NameBusiness QuestionKey Technique
Q1vw_HotelOccupancyRateOccupancy % per hotel (last 30 days)COUNT DISTINCT · NULLIF guard
Q2vw_MonthlyRevenueRevenue trend by month & hotelYEAR/MONTH · GROUP BY
Q3vw_GuestStayHistoryGuest lifetime value & stay countLEFT JOIN · SUM · COUNT
Q4vw_CancellationByChannelCancellation rate per booking channelCorrelated subquery · CAST %
Q5vw_RoomTypePerformanceRevenue & demand by room typeLEFT JOIN · AVG · SUM
Q6vw_OutstandingInvoicesUnpaid balances & days overdueDATEDIFF · WHERE outstanding > 0
Q7vw_ServiceUsageAnalysisMost revenue-generating servicesLEFT JOIN Services · rank
Q8vw_PaymentMethodBreakdownRevenue share per payment methodScalar subquery · % share
Q9vw_HousekeepingTurnaroundAvg & max cleaning time per roomDATEDIFF MINUTE · AVG · MAX
Q10vw_StaffPerformanceRevenue & bookings per staff memberMultiple LEFT JOINs · COUNT · SUM
EXPLORATORY DATA ANALYSIS

20-Question EDA Framework

Full Appendix B compliance — data quality, univariate, bivariate, and multivariate analysis on 119,390 records.

Q1–Q9: Data Quality Assessment

Data Quality Summary — Missing Values & Validity ViolationsQ4–Q8
Fig 6.1 — Missing values by column (left) and validity violations resolved (right). 99.8% data retained post-cleaning.
Data Cleaning DecisionsQ4–Q9
ColumnIssueActionRows
company94.3% missingDrop column112,593
agent13.7% missingFill = 016,340
country0.4% missingFill 'Unknown'488
children<0.1% missingFill = 04
Ghost bookings0 total guestsRemove180
ADR outliers>99th pctWinsorise~1,200
Long stays>30 nightsFilter~80

Q10–Q12: Numerical Distributions & Outliers

Lead Time DistributionQ11
Right-skewed: 70% of guests book within 100 days. Mean (104d) > Median (69d). Cancelled bookings average 145 days lead time vs 79 days for kept bookings.
Average Daily Rate (ADR) DistributionQ11
Right-skewed: ADR clusters €80–€110. Mean (€101.8) > Median (€94.6) — luxury outliers pull the mean. Capped at 99th percentile (€510) after cleaning.
Outlier Detection — BoxplotsQ12
Boxplots for lead time, ADR, and week nights. Treatment: ADR capped at 99th pct · Lead time kept (valid business) · Week nights capped at 30.

Q13–Q14: Categorical Analysis

Market Segment DistributionQ13
Online TA dominates at 47%. Rare categories (<3%) consolidated into 'Other'. Direct bookings (13%) offer best unit economics — higher ADR, lower cancellation.
Seasonal Booking Volume + ADR TrendQ13 / Q17
Dual-axis: booking volume (bars) and mean ADR (line) by month. Summer peak July/August — 35% above monthly average. ADR rises in parallel with demand.
EDA Summary Dashboard — Categorical Insights & Cross-Tab HeatmapQ13 · Q18 · Q19
Customer type mix, deposit type distribution, meal plan, and cancellation heatmap by hotel type × market segment. Online TA + City Hotel = highest cancel concentration.

Q15–Q16: Correlation & Relationships

Pearson Correlation MatrixQ15 · Q19
Lower triangle heatmap. Key: weekend/weeknights (r=0.62) and lead_time/is_canceled (r≈+0.22) are the top actionable signals.
Scatter Plots — Key Numerical PairsQ16
Weekend vs week nights (r=0.62): linear positive trend. Adults vs ADR (r=0.14): validates per-person pricing. Room type is the dominant ADR driver.

Q17–Q18: ADR Analysis & Cancellation Drivers

ADR by Hotel Type — Mean vs MedianQ17
Resort Hotels command 16% ADR premium (€112 vs €96). Gap widens in July/August. Both mean and median confirm — not driven by outliers alone.
Cancellation Driver AnalysisQ18
Non-Refund deposits: ~99% cancel rate (OTA paradox). Cancelled bookings: mean lead 145d vs 79d. Special requests inversely predict cancellations.
Cancellation Rate by Market SegmentQ18
Online TA and Groups show highest cancellation rates. Corporate and Direct channels are most stable. Colour coding: red >35%, amber 25–35%, green <25%.
Room Type PerformanceQ5 · Q17
Mean ADR and booking volume by room type. Room A dominates volume; premium types command higher ADR with lower demand.
Guest Origins — Top CountriesQ13
Portugal (41%) is the dominant origin — domestic travellers. UK (12%) and France (10%) are top international markets. Country drives seasonal and cancellation patterns.
Cancellation by Customer Type & HotelQ18
Transient-Party customers show highest cancellation rates. Contract customers are most stable. City Hotels experience slightly higher cancellation rates than Resort Hotels.
Special Requests & Meal Plan AnalysisQ13 · Q17
ADR rises with special request count — engaged guests pay more and cancel less. Full Board commands the highest ADR among meal plan categories.

KEY INSIGHT — THE NON-REFUND PARADOX
Non-Refundable deposits with lead time >120 days show a ~99% cancellation rate. OTA guests book speculatively at the cheapest rate, then cancel. This is not a data anomaly — it is a systemic policy vulnerability requiring fundamental redesign.

BUSINESS RECOMMENDATIONS

6 Actionable Strategies

Derived directly from EDA findings and validated by the ML classifier. Structured by implementation priority.

🔴 IMMEDIATE PRIORITY
Implement Real-Time Cancellation Risk Score
Integrate into PMS: lead_time>120d (+2pts) + Non-Refund deposit (+3pts) + prior_cancellations≥1 (+2pts). Score≥5 triggers re-confirmation workflow 14 days pre-arrival. AUC 0.86 validates signal accuracy.
Impact: Manage ~15% of high-risk inventory proactively
🔴 IMMEDIATE PRIORITY
Revise Non-Refundable OTA Deposit Policy
The ~99% cancellation rate for Non-Refund OTA bookings is a systemic policy failure. Negotiate true pre-auth captures with OTA partners or discontinue Non-Refund rate plans on OTA channels entirely.
Impact: Eliminate the deposit-cancellation paradox
🟡 MEDIUM-TERM
Launch Direct Booking Incentive Programme
Direct bookings deliver higher ADR and lower cancellation than any OTA channel. Offer guaranteed upgrade + flexible cancellation. A 5% OTA→Direct shift saves ~2.3pp cancellation rate + improves mean ADR.
Impact: −2.3pp cancel rate · higher ADR
🟡 MEDIUM-TERM
Special Request Solicitation at Booking
Inverse relationship: guests who make special requests cancel substantially less. Automated post-booking email asking for preferences (room floor, dietary, transport) deepens commitment. Low cost, measurable effect.
Impact: Reduce cancellation via psychological investment
🔵 STRATEGIC
Structured Loyalty Programme
Repeat guests cancel at 15% vs 40% — a 25pp differential. Converting 1 new → repeat guest eliminates 2.7× the cancellation risk. Tiered benefits (free nights, upgrade eligibility, priority check-in) drive conversion.
Impact: Each repeat conversion = 2.7× risk reduction
🔵 STRATEGIC
Power BI Revenue Management Dashboard
Deploy Power BI connected via DirectQuery to SQL Server using the 10 analytical views as the data layer. Live occupancy, ADR by room type, outstanding receivables, cancellation by channel, and staff performance — all unified.
Impact: Real-time operational decision support
IMPLEMENTATION PLAN

Site Update Roadmap

Professional 5-phase plan to upgrade the Vercel dashboard with all project content, new visualizations, and production-grade architecture.

SCOPE
Target: hotel-booking-dashboard-nine.vercel.app · Stack: Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS + Chart.js · Deployment: Vercel · Estimated total effort: ~40 engineering hours across 5 phases
PHASE 1 — DAYS 1–3
Foundation & Design System Upgrade
Replace current layout with the Navy/Teal/Amber design system used in the report and presentation. Set CSS variables, typography (Playfair Display + DM Sans), dark mode toggle, and sticky navigation with all sections linked.
CSS custom properties Playfair + DM Sans fonts Dark mode toggle Sticky nav + scroll-spy Hero section Responsive grid
PHASE 2 — DAYS 4–6
KPI Dashboard & Live Charts
Upgrade the Executive Intelligence section with 6 dynamic KPI cards, 6 Chart.js interactive charts (monthly trend, cancel by segment, ADR by season, meal plan, demographics, deposit type), and the risk matrix table with colour-coded cells.
6 KPI cards Chart.js 4.x Monthly trend chart Cancel segment chart ADR seasonal chart Risk matrix table Filter chips
PHASE 3 — DAYS 7–10
EDA Section — 18 Plot Gallery
Embed all 18 EDA plots as optimised WebP images with professional plot-card components (header, caption, Q-number badge). Add tabs for Q-categories: Data Quality, Distributions, Categorical, Correlation, Cancellation Drivers.
18 plot-card components Q-number badges Tab navigation Image lazy-loading Lightbox on click Figure captions
PHASE 4 — DAYS 11–14
ML Models & Database Documentation
Add the Orange workflow diagrams, feature importance chart, cluster visualization, and Silhouette score display. Upgrade the Database section with the SQL views table, CRUD examples in syntax-highlighted code blocks, and the ERD reference link.
Feature importance bars Cluster cards Orange workflow diagram SQL views table Code blocks (JetBrains Mono) ERD modal
PHASE 5 — DAYS 15–17
Content Polish & Vercel Deployment
Add scroll-reveal animations, all recommendation cards, team section, footer with logos, SEO meta tags, and Open Graph image. Optimize all images (<150KB each as WebP). Deploy to Vercel, verify all routes, run Lighthouse audit (target >90).
Scroll-reveal animations 6 recommendation cards Team section MTC + DiGiLiANS logos WebP image optimization SEO meta + OG Vercel deploy Lighthouse >90
PHASE 6 — DAYS 18–20 (OPTIONAL)
Advanced Enhancements
Connect live data via Supabase or Google Sheets API. Add a booking risk calculator widget where users input lead time, deposit type, and prior cancellations to get a live risk score. Embed Power BI iframe if available.
Supabase/Sheets API Risk score calculator Power BI embed Print-to-PDF mode
Recommended File Structure for Vercel DeploymentNext.js or Static
hotel-dashboard/ ├── index.html ← main entry point (this file) ├── css/ │ ├── style.css ← design system variables, layout │ └── charts.css ← chart & plot card styles ├── js/ │ ├── charts.js ← all Chart.js definitions │ ├── filters.js ← hotel/year filter logic │ └── animations.js ← scroll-reveal, transitions ├── images/ │ ├── plots/ ← p01–p18 EDA plots (WebP, <150KB each) │ ├── mtc-logo.png │ ├── digilians-logo.png │ └── og-image.png ← Open Graph social preview ├── data/ │ └── hotel_summary.json ← aggregated KPI data (no raw CSV needed) └── vercel.json ← headers, rewrites, cache config
